input.prompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

data.content:
`SafeAreaEdges="None"` stops the page from adding **safe-area padding**, but there are a few other things that commonly keep an image from truly going edge-to-edge:

**1. Hide the Shell/Navigation chrome**

```xml
<!-- If using Shell -->
<ContentPage Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"
             Shell.TabBarIsVisible="False" ... >

<!-- If using NavigationPage -->
<ContentPage NavigationPage.HasNavigationBar="False" ... >
```

The navigation bar and tab bar occupy space *above* the safe area. Even with `SafeAreaEdges="None"`, they still push your content down.

**2. Zero out the page's `Padding`**

```xml
<ContentPage Padding="0" ... >
```

Some styles or themes set a default `Padding` on `ContentPage`.

**3. Use `Aspect="AspectFill"` on the Image**

```xml
<Image Source="photo.jpg"
       Aspect="AspectFill"
       HorizontalOptions="Fill"
       VerticalOptions="Fill" />
```

`Aspect="AspectFit"` (the default) letterboxes the image, which looks like padding.

**Putting it all together:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             SafeAreaEdges="None"
             Padding="0"
             Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"
             Shell.TabBarIsVisible="False">

    <Image Source="photo.jpg"
           Aspect="AspectFill"
           HorizontalOptions="Fill"
           VerticalOptions="Fill" />

</ContentPage>
```

On **.NET 10 + Android 15**, the system draws edge-to-edge by default (transparent status/nav bars), so once you remove MAUI's own chrome and safe-area insets, the image will fill the entire screen. On **iOS**, `SafeAreaEdges="None"` similarly lets your content extend behind the notch and home indicator.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about .NET MAUI edge-to-edge display on .NET 10. This is a known topic — in .NET 10, MAUI introduced a new edge-to-edge display model (especially on Android). Let me think about what I know and what might help.

In .NET 10 (preview), .NET MAUI introduced edge-to-edge support. The key points:

1. `SafeAreaEdges="None"` on ContentPage tells the page not to add padding for safe areas, but that alone doesn't make the app go edge-to-edge.

2. On Android (.NET 10), edge-to-edge is the default behavior — the app draws behind system bars. But you need to make sure you're not inadvertently adding padding elsewhere.


3. The real issue is that `SafeAreaEdges` controls padding around system bars, but on Android 15+ with .NET 10, edge-to-edge rendering is enforced by default — so setting `SafeAreaEdges="None"` should theoretically work, though there might be other layout properties or platform-specific settings interfering with the expected behavior.

If they're still seeing padding despite that setting, the culprit is likely a `Shell` or `NavigationPage` wrapper adding its own chrome, or default margins/padding on the page or image container itself. For a true full-screen photo viewer, they'd need to hide the navigation bar and strip any padding from the page and layout hierarchy, plus ensure the image uses `AspectFill` or `Fill` to actually stretch edge-to-edge. I'm realizing the key issue is that in .NET 10, the page automatically applies padding based on safe area edges, so I need to explicitly set `Padding="0"` and hide the navigation bar to get a true full-screen image without any system UI intrusions.
